mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from periods of rate stability.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating household income expectations, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output, which compresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where industrial production falls below its 12-month trend, indicating the signal fails in late-cycle conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls above 200,000 signal robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge during strong payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0% year-over-year, inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, depressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation remains persistently above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates corporate earnings are driven by non-consumer factors, creating valuation fragility and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production coinciding with stable federal funds rates indicates an isolated supply-side shock that markets rapidly price in, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of supply shock instances during tightening cycles, or the signal fails to separate from baseline market noise in walk-forward validation.
